label: The Wizard's Well
subject: Recursion (code)
bossHook: Assemble a spell that descends the well, grabs the treasure at the bottom, and returns all the way back up.

vocab.descendSame: DESCEND  ·  cast on room (n)  ⚠ same n

intro-return.title: The Returning Gem
intro-return.hook: A spell doesn't just go down — it hands its result back UP, room by room. Cast it and watch the value return.
intro-return.winText: n hit 0, the wizard grabbed 💎, and it returned all the way back up the well. That return trip is recursion 'unwinding'.

learn.frame: A spell that dives down has to climb back — so where does its answer actually come from?

reveal.body: Each room paused, waiting on the room below it. Grabbing 💎 at the bottom let them finish in reverse — deepest first. That climb back up is the call stack UNWINDING.
reveal.inTheWild: Every recursive call is a paused frame; answers flow back up as each one returns.

base-case.title: The Place to Stop
base-case.hook: This spell descends… but never stops. Add a STOP block so it grabs the treasure at n = 0 instead of falling forever.
base-case.winText: The STOP block caught n = 0 — treasure grabbed, spell unwinds home. That stop is the BASE CASE.

failText.missing-base-case: No STOP block. The wizard fell past the bottom into the endless dark — STACK OVERFLOW. Every recursion needs a base case.

reveal.body: The STOP gave the spell one room where it does NOT descend again. Without a single non-recursive answer, nothing ever returns. That room is the BASE CASE.
reveal.inTheWild: factorial(0) = 1, an empty list, a tree leaf — one case answered without recursing.

insights.missing-base-case: A recursive spell keeps calling itself; the base case is the one branch that returns WITHOUT calling again. It's the only thing that can end the chain.

recursive-case.title: Marching Down
recursive-case.hook: You've got a STOP at n = 0 — but the spell has to actually MOVE toward it. Add a DESCEND that casts on a SMALLER room (n − 1).
recursive-case.winText: Each cast shrank n by one until STOP fired. Shrinking toward the base case is the RECURSIVE CASE.

failText.no-progress: Your DESCEND cast on the SAME room (n), so n never shrank and n = 0 never came. The recursive call must move toward the base case.

reveal.body: Every DESCEND handed down a SMALLER room, n − 1, so the spell crept toward STOP at n = 0. Shrinking the problem each call is the RECURSIVE CASE.
reveal.inTheWild: sum a shorter list, walk a child node, n − 1 — the recursive call always takes a smaller version.

insights.no-progress: The base case can only fire if the input keeps shrinking toward it. A call that passes the same problem down makes no progress — the exit is never reached.

boss-descent.title: BOSS · The Deep Well
boss-descent.hook: No blocks placed. Build the whole spell: a STOP at the bottom AND a DESCEND that shrinks. Four rooms deep. Go.
boss-descent.winText: Base case + shrinking recursive case, built from scratch. Boss down. That is recursion.

failText.missing-base-case: No STOP — it fell forever. STACK OVERFLOW.
failText.no-progress: It never shrank toward n = 0 — infinite descent.

learn.frame: Both halves now, no hints: what has to be true for a spell to fall all the way down AND return all the way up?

reveal.body: A STOP that answers at the bottom, plus a DESCEND that shrinks toward it — together that's the whole shape. Base case to end + recursive case that moves toward it IS recursion.
reveal.inTheWild: Every recursive function is these two parts: the case it answers directly, and the case that recurses on something smaller.

insights.missing-base-case: The base case is what makes the return trip possible — no bottom means no unwinding, just a stack that grows until it overflows.
insights.no-progress: The recursive case is the bridge to the base case; if it never shrinks the problem, the base case might as well not be there.

bottomless-pit.title: SIDE-QUEST · The Bottomless Pit
bottomless-pit.hook: You kept falling in. Let's drill ONE thing until it's automatic: place the STOP so the spell knows when to quit.
bottomless-pit.winText: STOP placed, pit escaped. The base case is the exit door — no recursion is safe without it.

failText.missing-base-case: Still no STOP — still falling. The base case has to be checked BEFORE the descend.

reveal.body: One STOP, checked first, and the falling ends. The base case is really just the question 'am I already done?' — asked before you descend again.
reveal.inTheWild: if (n === 0) return — the guard at the very top of almost every recursive function.

insights.missing-base-case: The base case is a decision, not an afterthought: each call asks 'am I done?' at the START and only descends if the answer is no.

heal-recursive-case.title: One Step Closer
heal-recursive-case.hook: Your STOP is set for n = 0 — the base case is ready. Now the recursive step must actually MOVE toward it. Pick the DESCEND that re-casts on a SMALLER room (n − 1) instead of the same one.
heal-recursive-case.winText: n went 2 → 1 → 0, and STOP fired at the bottom. A recursive step that shrinks n toward the base case IS the RECURSIVE CASE — that's the whole trick.

failText.no-progress: Your step re-cast on the SAME room (n stayed 2), so it never shrank and n = 0 never arrived — the well overflowed. The recursive case has to hand a SMALLER problem to the next call.

reveal.body: n went 2 → 1 → 0 because each step re-cast on a SMALLER room. That steady shrink toward the base case is the RECURSIVE CASE — the engine that gets you there.
reveal.inTheWild: recurse on n − 1, on the rest of the list, on the child — always a smaller piece than you got.

insights.no-progress: There has to be some measure — here, n — that strictly drops every call. That decreasing measure is the proof the recursion actually ends.
